concerns.text: Composite complexity score validation is circular: testing whether a weighted sum of predictors (crossing number + braid index) correlates with hyperbolic volume is equivalent to testing the regression model itself. This adds methodological complexity without clear justification beyond exploratory analysis.

concerns.location: research.md:Research Methodology Phase 1 Analytical Pipeline step 2
concerns.text: Additional invariants (arc index, Seifert circle count, bridge number) have known mathematical constraints with primary predictors (e.g., bridge number ≤ crossing number). Using them in regression without explicitly modeling these dependencies risks severe multicollinearity and misleading coefficient interpretation beyond the stated VIF > 5 flag.

Alternating/non-alternating classification is used for stratification in exploratory analysis, but regression modeling doesn't specify incorporation method (interaction terms, separate models, or control variable). This affects causal interpretation of the joint predictor relationship.

States 'complete data download and invariant computation for ≤13 crossing number dataset' but Phase 1 conclusions limited to ≤10 per spec. Methodology should clarify whether ≤13 data will be used for exploratory analysis or excluded from all Phase 1 conclusions.

For mathematical invariants (not predictive ML), 20% random stratified split 'validation sample' provides less meaningful validation than standard regression diagnostics. Spec acknowledges this but methodology should clarify this is correlation analysis, not predictive model validation.
